backlog.title: Author full OIDC/OAuth2 provider contracts in auth.tsp + auth.asyncapi.yaml (generator-first owner)
backlog.generator_first_target: Extend auth.tsp with discovery (.well-known/openid-configuration), JWKS (.well-known/jwks.json), /authorize, /token (grant_type union: authorization_code, refresh_token, client_credentials, device_code, urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer), /userinfo, /introspect (RFC 7662), /revoke (RFC 7009), /end-session, /device, /par, and oidc_clients CRUD. Regenerate @curaos/identity-sdk. Feature lands in TypeSpec FIRST per generator-evolution-rule; emitServiceLive wires the module; oidc_clients registration is a contract-driven seed.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Resource-oriented, tenant-scoped (nid/Citus), person-readable. Same contract drives both person-facing account/consent surfaces and org-admin management surfaces - dual surface from one contract.
backlog.license_status: All-ours toolchain (TypeSpec + AsyncAPI + @hey-api/openapi-ts). No foreign code.
backlog.acceptance: tsp compiles; @curaos/identity-sdk regenerates clean; contract review approved; every provider/oidc_clients surface present in the spec graph.

backlog.title: Stand up OIDC provider module (node-oidc-provider in NestJS) behind CURAOS_OWN_OIDC flag
backlog.generator_first_target: emitServiceLive wires the module; Drizzle/Citus adapter (nid tenant-scoped) emitted from contract; provider config (discovery/JWKS/grants) generated from auth.tsp surface. Port-adapt Logto packages/core/src/oidc/* (adapter.ts, grants/*, scope.ts, extra-token-claims.ts, resource.ts, init.ts) as the template.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Provider tenant-scoped via nid (Citus); issuer/JWKS per-deployment, air-gap viable (zero external discovery calls). Reuses existing ES256 keyset, refresh-session store (as refresh-token backend), DPoP, webauthn + MFA + lockout as interaction impls so the person keeps every credential they already own.
backlog.license_status: VENDOR node-oidc-provider (MIT). PORT-ADAPT Logto oidc/* patterns (MPL-2.0, patterns not files). REFERENCE Hydra/pocket-id (Apache-2.0/BSD-2) for correctness.
backlog.acceptance: Provider boots; discovery + JWKS endpoints serve the existing ES256 keyset with key-rotation window; authorization_code + PKCE round-trip conformance smoke green locally; ships dark (flag off).

backlog.title: Wire /token grants + DPoP + refresh-session reuse + key rotation
backlog.generator_first_target: grant_type dispatch emitted from contract; provider token grants reuse existing login-token.service ES256 signing + refresh-session.service rotation as the adapter backend so revocation/listing stays unified. Add device_code + client_credentials + private_key_jwt/client_secret_jwt client auth. Reuse dpop-replay-store pattern for client-assertion JTI dedup.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Refresh-token backend is the same store the person sees + revokes in their account console - no parallel session universe. DPoP cnf binding preserved so tokens stay sender-constrained for the person's protection.
backlog.license_status: Local code reuse + node-oidc-provider (MIT) grant machinery.
backlog.acceptance: authorization_code, refresh_token, client_credentials, device_code grants all mint valid ES256 tokens with DPoP cnf; client_assertion JTI replay blocked; refresh rotation routes through existing refresh-session store; revoke/introspect resolve.

backlog.title: Userinfo + introspection + revocation + end-session (front/back-channel logout) endpoints
backlog.generator_first_target: /userinfo, /introspect, /revoke, /end-session handlers emitted from contract; claims mapped from existing user model + @curaos/policy roles via a claims/scope mapper (mine Logto scope.ts + extra-token-claims.ts). backchannel_logout_uri per client persisted in oidc_clients.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: end-session cascades to refresh-session revocation so a person logging out of one client can cascade-kill sessions; back-channel logout notifies every client the person was in. Userinfo claims are exactly what the person consented to share.
backlog.license_status: node-oidc-provider (MIT) provides all three; Logto scope/claims patterns (MPL-2.0, patterns only).
backlog.acceptance: /userinfo returns OIDC claim shape from user model + roles; /introspect (RFC 7662) + /revoke (RFC 7009) conformant; end_session with id_token_hint cascades refresh-session revocation; back-channel logout fires per client.

backlog.title: OIDC client (relying-party) registry: oidc_clients table + CRUD admin routes + secret hashing
backlog.generator_first_target: oidc_clients table + CRUD + per-client registration emitted into contract as a contract-driven seed (generator-first). Secret hashing reuses argon2 password-hasher. Fields: client_id, redirect_uris, logout_uris, PKCE flag, skip-consent, allowed_user_groups gate, backchannel_logout_uri, client auth method.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Client list is org-admin-scoped (a customer-org admin manages only their org's clients), not god-mode. allowed_user_groups gate is org-scoped access control.
backlog.license_status: Local; data-model shape mined from pocket-id oidc/* (BSD-2 reference) + Logto (MPL-2.0 pattern).
backlog.acceptance: oidc_clients CRUD works with argon2-hashed secrets, secret rotation, PKCE flag, callback/logout URLs, skip-consent, group gating; the 19-22 FE apps + broker consumer seedable as clients.

backlog.title: Provider interaction UI in hosted-login (consent, account-selection, reauth, device-verify)
backlog.generator_first_target: Interaction routes driven by the provider interaction API; wired to existing login-user.service + webauthn + MFA. Consent/authorize-interaction/account-selection/reauth/device-verification pages. Mine Logto experience package patterns.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person-centric consent screen shows the person EXACTLY what each client receives and lets them revoke later from the account console. Recovery via code OR passkey keeps the person unlocked. Per-org branding on the journey.
backlog.license_status: Logto experience patterns (MPL-2.0, patterns). @simplewebauthn (MIT) already in use.
backlog.acceptance: Full browser authorization_code login through hosted-login works locally with real DB-seeded data; consent screen renders scopes; passkey + MFA reachable as interaction steps; device-verification page resolves user_code.

backlog.title: Surface passkey/WebAuthn as authorize-flow interaction step + add credential rename/label
backlog.generator_first_target: Add rename/label op to credential-management routes already in auth.tsp; surface passkey as an interaction step inside the new /authorize flow (currently only direct POST).
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Credentials are already person-owned; add per-device naming + last-used so the person manages their own keys. Admin surface is read-only for support.
backlog.license_status: HAVE locally. @simplewebauthn (MIT). pocket-id go-webauthn device-state shape (BSD-2 reference).
backlog.acceptance: Passkey registration + login reachable inside /authorize; rename/label persists; last-used surfaced in account console; existing direct-POST paths preserved.

backlog.title: Custom claims (per-user + per-group) + profile picture + emailVerified claim + claims mapper
backlog.generator_first_target: custom_claims table (per-user + per-group) + claims mapper emitted from contract; profile-picture upload route to @curaos/storage; emailVerified column surfaced as a claim; ldapId column added for federation linkage.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person owns their profile + picture; per-group claims attach org context. Claims mapper injects only what the person consented to.
backlog.license_status: Local; CustomClaim shape mined from pocket-id (BSD-2 reference).
backlog.acceptance: Per-user + per-group custom claims inject into tokens via mapper; profile picture uploads to @curaos/storage; emailVerified appears as a claim; ldapId column present.

backlog.title: Email verification + signup-token usage limits + unauthenticated one-time-access-email
backlog.generator_first_target: email-verification token + send/verify routes emitted from contract (via @curaos/providers EmailProvider); extend registration tokens (registration-store.ts) with usageLimit/usageCount + default-claims; unauthenticated one-time-access-email request endpoint.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Self-service: the person verifies their own email and can request a one-time access email while locked out - keeps the person unlocked without an admin.
backlog.license_status: Local; pocket-id user_signup_service.go (BSD-2 reference) + Logto (MPL pattern).
backlog.acceptance: send/verify email-verification routes work via EmailProvider; signup tokens enforce usageLimit/usageCount + apply default claims; unauthenticated one-time-access-email endpoint delivers + redeems.

backlog.title: API keys (user personal access tokens): api_keys table + CRUD + middleware + expiry email
backlog.generator_first_target: api_keys table + CRUD + verify middleware emitted from contract; scoped, expiry, lastUsedAt, expiration email via @curaos/providers EmailProvider; account-console panel.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person creates + scopes + revokes their own PATs from the account console; expiration email warns the person before lockout. client_credentials covers machine-to-machine; user PAT is the person's own automation key.
backlog.license_status: Local; pocket-id apikey/* (BSD-2 reference) + supertokens (Apache-2.0 reference).
backlog.acceptance: api_keys CRUD with scope + expiry; middleware authenticates PATs; lastUsedAt updates; expiration email fires; account-console panel lists/revokes.

backlog.title: Initial admin setup (passkey-based first-run bootstrap) + setup lock
backlog.generator_first_target: One-shot /setup route emitted from contract: registers the first admin via passkey, then locks; reuses break-glass audit.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Passwordless first-run: the first admin is a person with a passkey, not a shared secret. Break-glass audit records the bootstrap.
backlog.license_status: Local; pattern reference pocket-id initial-admin (BSD-2).
backlog.acceptance: /setup registers first admin via passkey then refuses further setup; break-glass audit entry written; idempotent lock proven.

backlog.title: Audit: feed OIDC provider events into hash-chain + optional geo enrichment
backlog.generator_first_target: Provider events (authorize/consent/token/end-session) feed the existing tamper-evident hash-chain + outbox; add optional geo enrichment (provider, GeoLite2); audit-log query API already in audit-event.schema.ts.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person sees their OWN security activity (account console activity/security-overview already built); org-admins see org-scoped audit; tamper-evidence protects the person's record.
backlog.license_status: HAVE locally. Geo enrichment via provider. Zitadel audit completeness (AGPL reference only).
backlog.acceptance: Every authorize/consent/token/end-session event lands in the hash-chain; geo fields populate when enabled; query API returns provider events; chain verifies.

backlog.title: B2B Organization model (org-within-tenant): organization/org_role/org_scope/org_invitation/email_domain
backlog.owner_path: identity-service (new B2B org layer) + @curaos/policy (org-roles) + @curaos/tenancy (unchanged isolation boundary)
backlog.generator_first_target: organization + org_role + org_scope + org_invitation + email_domain contracts emitted; org claims (org_id, org_roles) injected into tokens via the claims mapper; org-switch + JIT-domain-join as person-centric account actions. Port-adapt Logto Organizations model (exact stack + WorkOS-superset shape).
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Two layers both honored: @curaos/tenancy = deployment/isolation tenant (PHI never crosses); customer-org = the B2B grouping the PERSON belongs to. A person can belong to MULTIPLE orgs; org-switch is a person-centric account action; JIT email-domain join makes onboarding a person-journey, not an admin task.
backlog.license_status: PORT-ADAPT Logto org model (MPL-2.0, pattern, our stack). REFERENCE Zitadel Instance->Org->Project (AGPL) + Keycloak Organizations (Apache-2.0).
backlog.acceptance: org CRUD + org_role/org_scope assignment + org_invitation flow + email_domain JIT SSO; org_id + org_roles in token claims for the active org; person can belong to + switch between multiple orgs; @curaos/tenancy isolation boundary untouched.

backlog.title: Org-scoped RBAC: org-roles + composite roles + scope claims in token mapper
backlog.generator_first_target: role + scope + role-scope + composite contracts emitted; role claims injected via the token mapper. Extend @curaos/policy; port-adapt Logto org-role/scope -> token-claim path + Keycloak composite-roles.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Roles are org-scoped (B2B): a person has different roles in different orgs. Token carries org_roles for the active org. Person-facing apps see capabilities, not raw roles.
backlog.license_status: @curaos/policy is OURS (clean-room, ADR-noted no GPL/AGPL/MPL lift). References Logto (MPL) + Keycloak (Apache-2.0) patterns only.
backlog.acceptance: org-roles + composite roles assignable; scope claims appear in tokens for the active org; @curaos/policy engine resolves org-scoped permissions; field-level permlevels preserved.

backlog.title: Social federation as productized connectors (repurpose oidc-broker for BYO-external-OIDC)
backlog.generator_first_target: connector contract + per-connector config emitted; provider abstraction via @curaos/providers; port-adapt Logto connector-kit abstract-class pattern. The existing broker (already validates external OIDC + JWKS fetch + JTI replay) is the seam - keep it for BYO-external-OIDC after first-party cutover.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person can link/unlink external identities to their CuraOS account (account console). Org can pin which providers its members may use. Account-linking by email matches the broker's deriveActorId stability.
backlog.license_status: PORT-ADAPT Logto connector-kit (MPL-2.0, pattern). REFERENCE Kratos/authentik/keycloak (Apache-2.0/MIT).
backlog.acceptance: At least one social connector (Google/GitHub) links to an existing actor via deriveActorId; link/unlink works in account console; per-org allowed-providers enforced; broker repurposed for external-OIDC only.

backlog.title: MFA completion: TOTP + SMS/email OTP + AAL step-up (amr/acr claims)
backlog.generator_first_target: mfa factor types + AAL claim (amr/acr) in contract; challenge ops emitted; SMS/email delivery via @curaos/providers. Port-adapt Kratos AAL session model for step-up.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person chooses + manages their factors in the account console; org policy can REQUIRE a minimum AAL but the person keeps control of WHICH methods. Tenant policy via @curaos/policy.
backlog.license_status: Local extend. REFERENCE Kratos/Zitadel (Apache-2.0/AGPL, patterns) + Logto (MPL).
backlog.acceptance: TOTP + SMS/email OTP factors enroll + verify; recovery codes preserved; step-up triggers on acr_values; amr/acr claims appear in tokens; org min-AAL policy enforced.

backlog.title: Admin console app (clients/groups/claims/audit viewer) via generator emitUiApp
backlog.owner_path: new frontend admin app + identity-service admin API
backlog.generator_first_target: admin REST surface in contract; emitUiApp generates the admin app from @curaos/api-client hooks + @curaos/ui. Reference Logto/Keycloak admin IA for screen inventory.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Admin is ORG-admin-scoped, not god-mode: a customer-org admin manages only their org's members/roles/clients/audit. Screens organize around onboarding/serving the org's people, not raw CRUD.
backlog.license_status: Generator-emitted (ours). REFERENCE Logto console (MPL) + Keycloak admin-ui (Apache-2.0).
backlog.acceptance: Admin app emitted by emitUiApp; org-scoped clients/groups/claims/audit screens function against real DB-seeded data; no cross-org leakage.

backlog.title: Account console additions: authorized-OIDC-clients view/revoke + API-keys panel + email-verify + identity-linking
backlog.generator_first_target: Account routes already contract-backed; add authorized-clients list + revoke (uses provider grant store), API-keys panel, email-verify, identity-linking panels.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: THIS is the person-centric surface - the person owns profile, credentials, sessions, consents, exports, deletion, authorized clients, API keys, linked identities. Already exceeds pocket-id on GDPR (data-export/deletion).
backlog.license_status: HAVE-grade locally. REFERENCE Logto account (MPL) + others (Apache-2.0/MIT).
backlog.acceptance: Person can list + revoke authorized OIDC clients; manage API keys; verify email; link/unlink identities - all from the account console with real data.

backlog.title: LDAP/AD federation adapter (@curaos/identity-federation)
backlog.generator_first_target: ldap_source config contract + sync-trigger op emitted; ldapId column added to user model (IDM-8 dependency). TS polling-sync adapter (vendor ldapjs) via scheduler + @curaos/providers. Reference Keycloak attribute-mapping/sync semantics.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: LDAP is a per-org directory source for B2B tenants; synced users land in the org with org-scoped roles. Soft-delete = disable not remove (person data preserved). Self-hosted customer LDAP, no cloud directory.
backlog.license_status: VENDOR ldapjs (MIT). REFERENCE Keycloak federation/ldap (Apache-2.0) + authentik cmd/ldap (MIT) + pocket-id ldap_service.go (BSD-2).
backlog.acceptance: ldap_source config persists; polling sync imports users/groups/membership with attribute mapping; soft-delete disables not removes; admin-group detection works; per-org binding enforced.

backlog.title: SAML 2.0 (IdP + SP/source) connector (@curaos/identity-federation)
backlog.generator_first_target: saml_application + saml_connector config contracts in TypeSpec; provider/connector config emitted per-org. Port-adapt Logto TS SAML; vendor a TS SAML lib (samlify family) for XML-DSig. Reference Keycloak saml-core for assertion/signature/SLO correctness.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: SAML is a per-org SSO source (B2B): each org configures its own IdP; person federates in and lands in their org context.
backlog.license_status: VENDOR samlify family (MIT). PORT-ADAPT Logto SAML (MPL-2.0). REFERENCE Keycloak saml-core (Apache-2.0).
backlog.acceptance: Per-org SAML connector config; SP-initiated + IdP-initiated login work; assertion signature verifies; SLO cascades; validated against Keycloak reference assertions.

backlog.title: SCIM 2.0 inbound provisioning (@curaos/identity-scim)
backlog.generator_first_target: SCIM resource schemas + endpoints in contract (a REST surface over the user/org model). Inbound Users+Groups CRUD + filter/bulk per RFC 7644. Reference Keycloak scim/* + Zitadel internal/api/scim.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: SCIM provisions org members for B2B tenants - an IT-admin convenience that still results in person-owned accounts.
backlog.license_status: REFERENCE Keycloak scim/* (Apache-2.0) + authentik providers/scim (MIT) + Zitadel scim (AGPL reference only).
backlog.acceptance: Inbound SCIM Users + Groups CRUD over user/org model; filter + bulk per RFC 7644; provisioned users land as org members; outbound reverse-sync filed forward.

backlog.title: Fine-grained entitlements / ReBAC (Zanzibar tuple model + Check) in @curaos/policy
backlog.owner_path: @curaos/policy extension (Postgres relation-tuple table + Check) - OpenFGA escape-hatch
backlog.generator_first_target: authorization_model DSL + relation_tuple + Check/ListObjects contracts emitted; tuples tenant-scoped (nid). Port-adapt OpenFGA Zanzibar tuple model + Check algorithm INTO @curaos/policy on Citus/Postgres (single-stack default). Benchmark Check latency; promote to embedded OpenFGA only if it fails.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Entitlements answer 'can THIS person do THIS to THIS resource' - the core person-centric authz question. Resource-ownership tuples make the person the owner of their records by default; sharing is an explicit person-driven grant.
backlog.license_status: PORT-ADAPT OpenFGA model/algorithm (Apache-2.0, pattern OK to lift). REFERENCE ory-keto (Apache-2.0). @curaos/policy is OURS.
backlog.acceptance: relation_tuple table + Check/ListObjects on Citus, tenant-scoped by nid; SDK exposes Check; latency benchmarked; OpenFGA promotion path documented if benchmark fails; RBAC+ABAC unaffected.

backlog.title: Session theft-detection (rotating-refresh) + OAuth2 code/interaction/device/PAR session store
backlog.generator_first_target: Provider Drizzle adapter persists code/interaction/device/PAR sessions; reuse existing refresh-session store as refresh backend; port-adapt supertokens rotating-refresh theft detection. Session contracts (list/revoke) already present; add theft-detection.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person sees + revokes every session/device in the account console (already built). Per-org session lifespans (Kratos pattern) let stricter orgs shorten without affecting the person's other orgs.
backlog.license_status: Local extend. REFERENCE supertokens/Hydra/Kratos (Apache-2.0, patterns).
backlog.acceptance: code/interaction/device/PAR sessions persist in adapter; refresh rotation detects token theft + revokes the family; per-org session lifespans honored; account-console session list shows all.

backlog.title: Per-tenant sign-in-experience / OIDC app-config KV store + branding
backlog.generator_first_target: per-tenant oidc/sign-in-experience config emitted from contract; branding -> theming; SMTP/signup/feature toggles via Unleash + @curaos/secrets + @curaos/providers. Mine Logto sign-in-experience.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Sign-in experience is per-org branded so the person sees their org's identity during the journey; feature toggles are tenant-scoped.
backlog.license_status: Local. REFERENCE Logto sign-in-experience (MPL pattern).
backlog.acceptance: Per-tenant sign-in-experience config drives hosted-login branding + enabled methods; SMTP/signup/feature toggles resolve per tenant; air-gap safe.

backlog.title: Token-claims hook (org/role/tenant enrichment) via event-led outbox + workflow-core
backlog.generator_first_target: token-hook config + event contracts (already event-led). Port-adapt Hydra token-hook for claims enrichment (resolve org/roles/tenant at token mint); for user-event side-effects prefer the existing event-led outbox + workflow-core (charter: configure not code) over a bespoke webhook engine.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Hooks enrich tokens with the person's active-org context; user-event flows (post-register onboarding) become person-journey BPM workflows.
backlog.license_status: Local + workflow-core (ours). REFERENCE Hydra token-hook (Apache-2.0) + Logto hooks (MPL).
backlog.acceptance: Token mint resolves + injects active-org context; post-register onboarding runs as a workflow-core flow via the outbox; no bespoke webhook engine added.

backlog.title: CUTOVER step 1-2: register clients + migrate users/passkeys/groups to our provider
backlog.generator_first_target: oidc_clients seed (contract-driven) for the 19-22 FE apps + broker consumer using the SAME client_ids/redirect_uris pocket-id used (nothing changes for the apps). Migration scripts emitted as service-owned fixtures.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Migration keeps deriveActorId (deterministic UUIDv5 keyed on subject) so every existing PERSON resolves to the SAME actor id - their data, sessions, consents survive. Passkeys migrate by shape-compatible export (credentialId, publicKey, transports, backup state).
backlog.license_status: Local migration code. pocket-id DB export (BSD-2 data, our extraction).
backlog.acceptance: Every existing user resolves to the same actor id; every registered passkey verifies against our provider; groups/claims migrated; client list complete. Operator step: snapshot pocket-id DB; run migration on canary tenant; diff actor-id mapping = zero drift.

backlog.title: CUTOVER step 3: switch the broker to OUR issuer (reversible canary)
backlog.generator_first_target: Repoint oidc-broker-config idpIssuer/idpAudience/JWKS at OUR provider (curaos-identity issuer) instead of auth.example.com/pocket-id. Broker keeps working unchanged (it just validates id_tokens against a discovery JWKS - now ours). This is the reversible seam.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Apps and people see no change - same login journey, same actor ids; only the issuer behind the curtain changes.
backlog.license_status: Config change only.
backlog.acceptance: Broker validates OUR id_tokens; FE apps log in end-to-end against our provider; live verification on a canary tenant. Operator step: flip issuer config on canary; verify-before-build proof (render+route+data); rollback = flip issuer back to pocket-id (proven before proceeding).

backlog.title: CUTOVER step 4: move apps to direct login + collapse/repurpose the broker
backlog.generator_first_target: Once apps log in directly against our provider, the broker becomes a no-op (our id_token IS already a CuraOS token shape, or /token mints the downstream shape directly). Delete src/auth/oidc-broker/* + /auth/oidc/exchange, OR keep it solely for genuine BYO-external-OIDC federation (its real long-term purpose, per IDM-15).
backlog.person_tenant_centric: Person login goes direct to our provider; broker survives only as the external-IdP federation seam, never in the first-party path.
backlog.license_status: Local refactor.
backlog.acceptance: No app depends on the broker for first-party login; broker repurposed to external-IdP-only or removed. Operator step: roll apps to direct login one at a time off the canary; confirm zero broker hits for first-party login over a soak window.

backlog.title: CUTOVER step 5: decommission pocket-id (DESTRUCTIVE - user-confirm)
backlog.generator_first_target: Drain auth.example.com traffic; snapshot pocket-id DB for audit retention; tear down pocket-id deployment + DNS; remove CURAOS_OIDC_* pocket-id env. Keep the snapshot per audit-retention policy.
backlog.person_tenant_centric: No person loses access - all identities already live on our provider; pocket-id is a drained shell at teardown.
backlog.license_status: N/A (teardown).
backlog.acceptance: Zero traffic to pocket-id for the soak window; rollback issuer-flip proven before teardown; user-confirmed (DESTRUCTIVE). Operator steps in order: (1) confirm zero traffic over soak; (2) DB snapshot to audit-retention store; (3) prove issuer-flip rollback still works; (4) USER CONFIRMS destructive teardown; (5) tear down deployment + DNS; (6) remove pocket-id env vars; (7) retain snapshot per policy.

license_register.rationale: Permissive; the actual OIDC/OAuth2 provider library (the lib Logto forks). Vendor as the direct dependency wrapped in NestJS. No restrictions on linking/distribution.

license_register.source: Logto packages/core/src/oidc/* + Organizations + connector-kit + experience + console/account + sign-in-experience + TS SAML
license_register.rationale: MPL-2.0 is file-level copyleft and service-boundary-safe but we do NOT lift files. Logto is the closest stack-fit corpus (TS, same node-oidc-provider, exact org model). Port-adapt the data model, endpoint shapes, claims mapper, org/scope->token-claim path, and connector abstract-class PATTERNS into our own clean code. Document pattern-reuse in the ADR; keep zero MPL-licensed files in our tree.

license_register.source: Ory Hydra (/oauth2 + /consent + Fosite) + Kratos (flow state-machines + AAL + webhook hooks) + Keto (Zanzibar + OPL)
license_register.verdict: REFERENCE (Hydra/Keto also vendorable as escape-hatch services)
license_register.rationale: Apache-2.0 is fully reuse-friendly. Hydra is the documented escape-hatch IdP if certification pressure outweighs single-stack (vendor as Go service then). Keto is the ReBAC escape-hatch. For v1/v1.1 we REFERENCE Hydra grant/flow/token-hook correctness, Kratos AAL + flow patterns, and Keto OPL only - no Go runtime added by default.

license_register.source: pocket-id /backend/internal/oidc/* + apikey/* + ldap_service.go + user_signup_service.go + go-webauthn data model + custom-claim + geo-audit
license_register.verdict: REFERENCE (port-adapt data model, no code lift)
license_register.rationale: BSD-2 is permissive and vendor-safe, but pocket-id is Go+Svelte+Fosite+GORM - wrong stack. We port-adapt the lean endpoint set, data model (oidc_clients, api_keys, custom_claims, signup-token usage limits, credential device-state), and audit geo-enrichment PATTERNS, not code. pocket-id is the capability-parity yardstick we must beat.

license_register.source: OpenFGA (model DSL, Check/ListObjects/ListUsers/Expand, contextual tuples, weighted-graph, Postgres backend)
license_register.verdict: PORT-ADAPT model/algorithm (Apache-2.0 permits lift) + VENDOR as escape-hatch service
license_register.rationale: Apache-2.0 permits lifting the Zanzibar tuple model + Check algorithm pattern INTO @curaos/policy on Citus/Postgres (single-stack default). If Check latency benchmarks fail, embed OpenFGA as a Go service (escape-hatch). Either path is license-clean (no GPL/AGPL/MPL).

license_register.source: Keycloak (services/protocol/saml + saml-core, federation/ldap + kerberos, Organizations, composite roles, scim/*, authz/UMA, admin-ui)
license_register.rationale: Apache-2.0 is reuse-friendly but Keycloak is Java - wrong stack. Reference for SAML assertion/signature/SLO correctness, LDAP attribute-mapping/sync semantics, Organizations + composite-role + SCIM + authz/UMA shapes, and admin-console IA. No code lifted into the TS stack.

license_register.source: authentik (providers/saml + sources/saml + sources/ldap + cmd/ldap outpost + providers/scim + Flow+Stage engine + Event)
license_register.verdict: REFERENCE (MIT-vendorable if a TS port were ever needed)
license_register.rationale: MIT is fully permissive but authentik is Python/Go - wrong stack. Reference the LDAP outpost sync model, SCIM outbound shape, SAML source patterns, the Flow+Stage composition engine (deferred), and event sanitization/retention. No runtime added.

license_register.source: Zitadel (Instance->Org->Project, Human/Machine + lifecycle state machine, internal/api/scim, internal/command/session.go AAL, Action flows, event-sourced audit, LDAPConfig)
license_register.verdict: REFERENCE ONLY (no code lift, no derivative)
license_register.rationale: AGPL is network-copyleft and INCOMPATIBLE with our charter; treat as conceptual REFERENCE ONLY - we read the org-hierarchy, machine-account, lifecycle-state-machine, per-factor AAL session, full-audit, and SCIM CRUD CONCEPTS and re-implement clean. Never copy any Zitadel file or produce an AGPL derivative.

license_register.source: supertokens (AuthRecipe primary-user + account-linking, TOTP/passwordless, rotating-refresh theft-detection, dashboard)
license_register.rationale: Apache-2.0 reuse-friendly. Reference the account-linking primary-user model (multiple credentials -> one primary, matches our deriveActorId stability) and rotating-refresh theft-detection pattern; re-implement on our stack.
Permissive; vendor as the LDAP client for the @curaos/identity-federation TS polling-sync adapter (v1.1).
Permissive; vendor a TS SAML library for XML-DSig rather than hand-rolling signing/verification in the @curaos/identity-federation SAML connector (v1.1).
Permissive; already the WebAuthn lib family in identity-service. No foreign code lifted - extend local usage.

CuraOS-owned. @curaos/policy is ADR-noted clean-room (no GPL/AGPL/MPL lift). Extend these as the canonical owners; all generator-first targets land here. The toolchain (TypeSpec + AsyncAPI + @hey-api/openapi-ts) is ours and references only permissive corpora.
